title: campanula thaliana var. ruthenica
intro: Campanula thaliana var. ruthenica, also known as the Harebell or Bellflower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula thaliana var. ruthenica include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
